LAW AND MOTION, DEPT, 302, AS TO DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF WONDWOSSEN MEKBEB, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, BUT WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO MR. MEKBEB SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND IF AND WHEN HE IS ABLE TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT ARE SUFFICIENT TO IMPOSE PUNITIVE DAMAGES LIABILITY ON UBER PER CIVIL CODE 3294(B). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Off calendar per the moving party by the notice it filed on May 16, 2018. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =(302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Tesfaye W. Tsadik, Tel. No. 1-510-639-3822 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Allison M. Lawrence of Tyson & Mendes, Tel. No. 1-628-253-5074 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Wondwossen Mekbeb is granted without leave to amend, but without prejudice to Mr. Mekbeb seeking leave to amend if and when he is able to allege facts that are sufficient to impose punitive damages liability on Uber per Civil Code 3294(b). Accepting the allegation that Uber is the employer of defendant Clarence Lalaind, Jr. as true, the FAC fails to allege sufficient facts to show any of the grounds on which an employer may be liable for punitive damages. Nor does it appear that it is reasonably possible for Mr. Mekbeb to allege such facts at this stage of the case. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
